top_classes.name: Sample No. 467, The Hunt and Salisbury Collection at the U.S.Geological Survey, Denver, Co.
Sample No. 390, The Hunt and Salisbury Collection at the U.S.Geological Survey, Denver, Co.
Sample No. 382, The Hunt and Salisbury Collection at the U.S.Geological Survey, Denver, Co.
Sample No. 358, The Hunt and Salisbury Collection at the U.S.Geological Survey, Denver, Co.

This sample probably represents a metamorphosed zircon syenite. It is a coarse-grained sample showing granulation along most grain boundaries. There are abundant anhedra of nepheline and microperthitic feldspar, an amphibole (either hornblende or a sodic phase), abundant magnetite and numerous grains of zircon. Cancrinite occurs aroundfeldspars scattered throughout the section. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.gneiss.coarse.gneiss4.spectrum.txt

This coarse-grained rock has been sheared enough to giveit mortar texture. The abundant albite subhedra (some with perthite) often evidence brittle deformation, while other areas have recrystallized. In addition to this phase there are muscoviteeuhedra (secondary to deformation), and larger primary muscovite heavily sheared to sericite in foliated masses or bands throughout the sample. Modes were 67% albite and 33% muscovite. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.gneiss.coarse.gneiss5.spectrum.txt

This is a sample much like the previous one in composition. It is a fine-grained slightly metamorphosed well-compacted sandstone with some secondary quartz deposition, and much more silica/chalcedony deposition. The quartz grains, unlike the previous rock, show evidence of only weak strain and fewer fluid inclusions. In addition there are scattered detrital green and brown tourmaline and zircon. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.quartzite.coarse.qrtzit5.spectrum.txt

Ooids in the sample are small (0.5-0.75 mm) with laminar structures largely destroyed by dolomitic recrystallization. Carbonate crystals show a heterogeneous distribution, from microcrystalline to fine medium-grained. Abundant quartz fragments and some altered plagioclase (albite twinning), opaques, carbonaceous material, ferruginous clay, and a brown-green to colorless micaceous mineral are also present. Particle size was 500 - 1500 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.sedimentary.limestone.coarse.limest7.spectrum.txt
Meerdink et al. 2019, Baldridge et al. 2009
